title: SEO Engine Success/Failure Rules — Phase 21
principle: The engine is judged by measurable results, not activity volume.

positive_signals.impressions_up_real: Impressions increase on changed real pages (7-14 day window after change)
positive_signals.position_improves: Average position improves on changed real pages
positive_signals.pages_get_clicks: Any page gets an organic click from search
positive_signals.pages_newly_indexed: Important discovered-not-indexed pages appear in indexed count
positive_signals.cannibalization_reduces: Thin tool/scenario pages lose rankings while parent calculators gain

negative_signals.impressions_decline: Changed pages lose impressions after 7-14 days
negative_signals.position_worsens: Average position worsens because wrong (thin) pages rank instead of parents
negative_signals.no_indexing_progress: No pages get indexed after upgrades are deployed for 7+ days
negative_signals.ctr_stays_zero: CTR = 0 after titles/CTR fixes are recrawled on pages with impressions
negative_signals.engine_activity_without_reason: Engine keeps changing pages without measurable reason or improvement signal
negative_signals.thin_variants_as_winners: Engine optimizes thin variants as if they were primary landing pages

measurement_window: 7-14 days after each change. Changes evaluated in weekly batches.
judgment_rule: If positive signals > negative signals after 14 days, engine is effective. If equal or negative, engine needs correction.
